title: Domain Errors, State, and Resource Lifecycles · Traceback Repair Bay
summary: Keep loan state transitions, storage sessions, and notification failures recoverable and observable. Learners isolate plausible failures, compare competing hypotheses, and record cause-level repair evidence. Planned artifact: Repair a changed failure in a stateful failure-resilient service and publish the reproduction, evidence, repair, and regression record. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Model allowed states and transitions explicitly so every method preserves domain invariants.
Define a small domain error hierarchy that communicates recoverable business failures without leaking infrastructure details.
Translate storage, network, and parsing failures at ownership boundaries while preserving causes for operators.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

options.text: Define the stakeholder decision, quantities, authority, and constraints
Choose and document the smallest defensible model or procedure
Execute the model while preserving inspectable intermediate evidence
Test boundaries and changed cases, communicate limits, and revise

steps.title: Publish the incident record
steps.instruction: Write a concise evidence note that explains when Oop Transaction Boundaries applies, what can fail, and how the changed case was verified.
steps.why: Retrieval and explanation expose gaps that recognition questions can hide.

content.title: Evidence defense
content.text: Use the case, one assumption, one observable result, and one limitation. Do not copy the competency statement: Group state changes into atomic application operations with clear success, rollback, and idempotency behavior.
